threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'last stop' and everyone else just gets up like it's their daily commute.
Overheard on the 59 last night: a couple arguing about how the sausage roll at the Bakerloo's kiosk is worth the late-night trek. They weren’t wrong.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Walking past the Kottbusser Tor one night, I heard a full-on orchestra practicing in an empty warehouse. That’s when I felt the heartbeat of this place.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can buy a fresh döner kebab at 4 AM after a night out, and it’s always the same guy behind the counter, friendly as ever.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their patience with a BART delay—only newcomers blame it on the Muni while waiting for a F train.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the bus while another passenger was trying to sell candy. Classic Muni—chaos and talent all in one cramped aisle.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to the Sol street for bars, but locals know the real vibe is in the quiet corners behind Carrer de Verdi, hidden gems.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell when someone's new by how they're still fumbling with the metro ticket machines, staring at them like it's a Rubik's cube.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you're new when you hesitate at the bus stop, wondering which direction the double decker will actually take you. Everyone else just knows.
Last night on the 133, a guy was reciting Shakespeare to a confused group of clubbers. Only in Brixton does poetry mix with late-night revelry.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a 70-year-old man in lederhosen selling homemade sauerkraut outside the U-Bahn station. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, where even the strobe lights seem to have a collective soul.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively dodge the BART delays like they're dodging a pothole—total second nature.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 31 pulling out a mini speaker to DJ a makeshift dance party.  Everyone was either vibing or pretending not to listen. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we get annoyed by their photos of Gaudí, but it's the ones asking for directions in bad Catalan that really test my patience.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily; they still use Google Maps for short walks instead of just asking the nearest bar owner for directions.
